description:
You are acting as a compliance-officer bot who has been asked to perform a very quick world-writable file sweep so that an internal auditor can check that no sensitive information is stored in files that can be modified by anyone.

1. Target tree  
   • The directory you must audit is  
     /home/user/audit_data  

2. What to look for  
   • World-writable **regular** files only (i.e. any file whose octal mode shows write-permission for “others”).  
   • Ignore directories, FIFOs, sockets, devices, etc.  

3. What you must create  
   • Create the directory /home/user/audit_report (if it does not already exist).  
   • Inside it, create a plain UTF-8 text file named world_writable_files.log.

4. Mandatory report format (this exact format will be used by the automated checker; do not add extra columns, headers, or blank lines):  
   Each world-writable regular file that exists anywhere under /home/user/audit_data **must** appear exactly once in world_writable_files.log, one file per line, in lexicographically ascending order.  
   The line format is:  

   <absolute_path>|<octal_permissions>|<size_in_bytes>  

   For example:  
   /abs/path/to/file|666|1234  

   • absolute_path  – full path starting with “/”.  
   • octal_permissions – the full 3-digit or 4-digit mode as reported by stat -c %a (e.g. 666, 0777, 1766).  
   • size_in_bytes – the decimal byte-size as reported by stat -c %s.  

5. Permissions  
   • The resulting world_writable_files.log file itself may be created with any reasonable default user permissions (e.g. 644).  
   • You do not need root access for this task.  

6. Complexity requirement  
   • The entire audit can be performed with a single shell command (though you may, of course, compose it as you see fit in the terminal).  

7. Completion check  
   The automated grader will:  
   a) Verify that /home/user/audit_report/world_writable_files.log exists.  
   b) Confirm that its content exactly matches the required format and that it lists all and only the world-writable regular files under /home/user/audit_data, sorted lexicographically.

Produce the report. Once the file is in place with the correct content, the task is complete.
truth:
Initial file system state (already present when the agent starts):

Directory /home/user/audit_data with the following structure:

/home/user/audit_data/secure.txt  
  • regular file  
  • size: 512 bytes  
  • permissions: 0644

/home/user/audit_data/public.log  
  • regular file  
  • size: 2048 bytes  
  • permissions: 0666   ← world-writable

/home/user/audit_data/notes  
  • regular file  
  • size: 256 bytes  
  • permissions: 0600

/home/user/audit_data/subdir  
  • directory  
  • permissions: 0755

/home/user/audit_data/subdir/expose.sh  
  • regular file  
  • size: 1024 bytes  
  • permissions: 0777   ← world-writable

Expected final artefact created by the agent:

/home/user/audit_report/world_writable_files.log  
Exact file content (newline-terminated Unix LF):

/home/user/audit_data/public.log|666|2048
/home/user/audit_data/subdir/expose.sh|777|1024

There must be no additional lines, headers, or trailing blank lines.  
The file must be present at that exact path for grading.
